title: Indicator Reference
description: Every indicator the engine computes, what it measures, and how to read it.

items.name: EMA (Exponential Moving Average)
items.formula: EMA_t = price * k + EMA_{t-1} * (1 - k),  k = 2 / (n + 1)
items.notes: Trend direction and dynamic support/resistance. Recent bars weigh more than old ones; the 20/50 pair defines the engine's trend read.

items.name: RSI (Relative Strength Index)
items.notes: Momentum oscillator, 0-100. Above 70 stretched, below 30 washed out - but in strong trends RSI stays pinned, so read it with the regime gate, never alone.

items.name: ATR (Average True Range)
items.formula: TR = max(high - low, |high - prev_close|, |low - prev_close|); ATR = avg(TR, 14)
items.notes: Volatility in price units. The engine sizes stop distances in ATR multiples (1.3-1.6x) so stops scale with conditions instead of feelings.

items.name: ADX (Average Directional Index)
items.notes: Trend strength, not direction. The engine's regime gate reads trending above 22, ranging below 16, with a volatile override at 1.7x median ATR%.

items.name: MACD
items.formula: MACD = EMA(12) - EMA(26); signal = EMA(MACD, 9); histogram = MACD - signal
items.notes: Momentum shifts via moving-average convergence. Histogram flips lead crossovers; both lag price by construction.

items.name: Bollinger Bands
items.notes: Volatility envelope. Touches are information, not signals - walks down the band are how crashes look.

items.name: VWAP
items.notes: The volume-weighted average participant entry. Institutional benchmark: above it buyers are in control on the session.

title: Order Types
description: What each order actually instructs the venue to do, and what can go wrong.

items.name: Market order
items.notes: Execute now at the best available price. Guarantees the fill, never the price - in thin books the slippage is yours.

items.name: Limit order
items.notes: Execute at your price or better. Guarantees the price, never the fill - the market can run without you.

items.name: Stop order
items.notes: Becomes a market order when the stop price trades. The standard exit mechanism; gaps can fill it far beyond the stop (see The Gap Down replay).

items.name: Stop-limit order
items.notes: Becomes a limit order at the stop. Caps slippage but can fail to fill entirely in a fast market - a stop that might not stop.

items.name: Trailing stop
items.notes: A stop that follows price by a fixed distance or percent. Locks in trend profits; too tight and ordinary noise takes you out.

items.name: OCO (one-cancels-other)
items.notes: A stop and a target bracket; whichever fills first cancels the other. This is the shape of a planned trade and the QuantGlass ticket's mental model.

title: Core Formulas
description: The arithmetic every plan rests on. All of these appear in lessons and live exercises.

items.name: Position size
items.notes: The only sizing formula. Risk is chosen first; size is derived, never the other way around.

items.name: R-multiple
items.notes: Results in units of initial risk. A -1R loss is a plan working; results only compare across trades in R.

items.name: Risk:reward ratio
items.notes: Bound before entry. At RR 2:1 you can be wrong 60% of the time and still grow.

items.name: Expectancy
items.notes: Average R per trade over many trades. Positive expectancy with survivable drawdowns is the entire game.

items.name: Breakeven win rate
items.notes: The win rate a given risk:reward needs just to tread water. At 1:1 it is 50%; at 3:1 only 25%.

items.name: Drawdown recovery
items.notes: A 50% drawdown needs a 100% gain to recover. The asymmetry is why risk caps exist.

title: Scam & Red-Flag Checklist
description: If any of these are present, the correct position size is zero.

items.name: Guaranteed returns
items.notes: Markets price risk. Anyone guaranteeing returns is either lying about the returns or hiding the risk.

items.name: Pressure to act now
items.notes: Real opportunities survive a day of thought. Urgency is a tool for preventing it.

items.name: Unsolicited contact
items.notes: Strangers with profitable secrets do not need you. Romance-to-investing pivots ('pig butchering') are an industrial-scale crime.

items.name: Pay to withdraw
items.notes: Any platform demanding fees or taxes before releasing 'your' money has already stolen it. Paying releases nothing.

items.name: Unverifiable track record
items.notes: Screenshots are free to fabricate. Audited, third-party-verified results or nothing.

items.name: Secret strategy
items.notes: Every legitimate edge can be described, even if not given away. 'Proprietary AI the SEC hates' is a plot, not a product.

items.name: Recruitment rewards
items.notes: When the returns come from new joiners, you are the product. That is the definition of a Ponzi structure.

items.name: Fake celebrity endorsement
items.notes: Deepfaked endorsements cost cents to make. No public figure is giving away crypto doubled-back.

items.name: Wallet 'validation' requests
items.notes: No legitimate service ever needs your seed phrase or private key. Sharing it is handing over the account.

items.name: Too-smooth equity curve
items.notes: Real trading has drawdowns. A curve that only goes up at 45 degrees is either fabricated or hiding tail risk that has not fired yet.
